items.title: Mark McGowan's right-hand man parachuted into plum cabinet role

children.content: New Landsdale MP Daniel Pastorelli, the former chief of staff to both Mark McGowan and Roger Cook, will be parachuted into cabinet to replace Paul Papalia, the ABC understands.

gallery.altText: Daniel Pastorelli looks into the distance while standing outside a building.

thumbnail.altText: Daniel Pastorelli looks into the distance while standing outside a building.

items.title: Dockers and Swans battle for top spot as Lions recall big guns

image.altText: A Fremantle AFL player props as he tries to evade an incoming Sydney Swans player.

children.content: It's first vs second to start AFL round 18, as Fremantle hosts the Swans in Perth, while the Lions recall big names in an ominous warning the competition. Follow live.

items.title: Record fine for gold miner thrusts tiny bat into spotlight

children.content: A Western Australian gold miner has been hit with a fine of more than $100,000 for failing to protect a vulnerable bat species.

image.altText: A small bat is held with two hands with its wings spread apart.

items.title: 'We'll sell his body parts': AI extortionists target missing man's parents

children.content: Scammers have sent the parents of a missing West Australian man an AI-generated image of their son and demanded thousands of dollars in exchange for his safety.

image.altText: A man with short dark hair and a close beard wearing a khaki green t-shirt sitting outside a playground.

items.title: Can Fremantle keep rolling to the minor premiership?

children.content: Fremantle's 14-game winning streak ended with a loss to GWS, but what are the keys for the Dockers to keep going and set up a tilt at their first flag? Cody Atkinson and Sean Lawson investigate.

gallery.altText: Four Fremantle AFL players (including three talls) stand in the rooms singing the club song after a win.
A serious-looking Fremantle AFL captain leads his dejected side off the field after a loss.
A Fremantle AFL player stretches his arms out above his head to take an athletic mark as a teammate stands by.

items.title: AFL's best two teams ready for titanic battle under Perth lights

children.content: Fremantle take on Sydney at Perth Stadium tonight, in a match boasting storylines as compelling as early-era Melrose Place. Who will emerge from the clash as premiership favourites?

image.altText: Luke Jackson celebrates an AFL goal with a Dockers teammate.

items.title: Water in the air an 'untapped water resource' until now

children.content: Can water be made from air in WA's Wheatbelt? Researchers are putting an invention to the test.

image.altText: A woman stands with solar panels behind her, she is looking up.

items.title: This nursery owner doesn't use Telstra, but the outage still cost her dearly

children.content: The economic cost of the latest Telstra network outage could run into hundreds of millions of dollars, an expert estimates.

articles.title: Telstra solves 'software defect' after hundreds of failed Triple Zero calls

children.content: Telstra has implemented a fresh "solution" to resolve a secondary software defect that left hundreds of customers unable to make Triple Zero calls during a nationwide network outage.

image.altText: A blurred woman with dark hair and a white top looking at a mobile phone in front of a clear Telstra orange and white logo.

thumbnail.altText: A woman in a beret stands surrounded by greenery in her nursery, staring at the camera

items.title: 'Bad mistake': ABC's editorial director questioned over inaccurate report

children.content: The ABC and SBS were called to give evidence at the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion, with the inquiry hearing that a number of complaints had been received about the public broadcasters' coverage of the conflict in the Middle East.

image.altText: Mr Fang wearing a suit and blue tie while walking down a street path

items.title: Perth house rents now the third most expensive in the country

children.content: Rents are soaring in several capital cities as low vacancy rates collide with rising interest rates and population growth.

image.altText: A for lease sign at a picket fence

items.title: Police call for help to handle 'disgusting' Facebook comments

children.content: The WA Police commissioner writes to Meta to ask for assistance with Facebook moderation after a number of derogatory comments are left on social media pages.

image.altText: A medium close-up shot of WA Police Commissioner Col Blanch in uniform.

items.title: Foreign investor snub of divestment order 'extraordinary', says expert

children.content: China-linked investors in a Western Australia rare earths company have yet to comply with federal Treasurer Jim Chalmers's latest order to sell their shares.

image.altText: A remote mining camp in the outback

items.title: BHP workers to strike as negotiations with mining giant stall

children.content: Unionised workers at the mining giant's operations in Port Hedland will down tools for eight hours on July 16.

items.title: 'Cannot remain silent': Egypt demands FIFA investigate World Cup referees

children.content: Egypt remains incensed at French referee Francois Letexier after his performance in the Round of 16 clash against Argentina at the FIFA World Cup.

image.altText: Mohamed Salah and the Egypt team argue with a referee

items.title: FIFA boss referred to Olympics ethics board over Trump World Cup call

children.content: Gianni Infantino's close ties with Donald Trump continue to land him in hot water after the US President's World Cup intervention.

image.altText: Donald Trump and Gianni Infantino standing with each other

items.title: Neil the seal 'may have moved on', Tasmanian wildlife experts say

children.content: Authorities in Tasmania say it appears Neil the seal may have left his resting spot at a Hobart beach and returned to sea.

image.altText: A five-year-old elephant seal known as Neil the Seal laying on a road at Seven Mile Beach, Tasmania.

items.title: SA confirms second H5 bird flu case, two more suspected in Perth and WA

children.content: South Australia confirms its second H5 bird flu case while Western Australia investigates two more suspected detections, as authorities say the virus has not spread beyond isolated wild birds.

image.altText: Clare scriven speaks into media microphones while Kyam Maher stands next to a map showing SA

items.title: Millions of tiny engineers help to restore health of marine ecosystem in WA

children.content: A restorative mussel farm could improve water quality and biodiversity in Cockburn Sound and the project leaders say there's no need to stop there.

items.title: Children's commissioner quits as CLP set to pass controversial welfare changes

children.content: Shahleena Musk's resignation comes as the Northern Territory government-dominated scrutiny committee backs the Country Liberal Party's changes to child protection laws, despite widespread opposition and confusion about a key aspect of the legislation.

image.altText: A woman wearing a black dress looking directly at the camera.

items.title: Dozens of Aboriginal language custodians call for further revival efforts

children.content: Aboriginal language custodians release a road map for the survival and revival of Northern Territory Indigenous languages.

image.altText: An over-the-shoulder shot of an Aboriginal child, blurred back of head in view, focus on hands holding a book.

items.title: How First Nations voices enrich Australia’s classical music traditions

children.content: From James Henry to Nardi Simpson, Aaron Wyatt, Adam Manning and Nina Korbe, here are First Nations voices making waves in Australia’s classical music scene.

image.altText: Soprano Nina Korbe on stage with QSO in a blue dress and arms outstretched singing into the QPAC Concert Hall.

items.title: Farmers search for ways to break free from controversial herbicide

children.content: Australia buys most of its paraquat from China, but is investigating ways to become less reliant on the chemical.

image.altText: A man wearing a cap, crouching in a paddock of wheat.

items.title: The 'amazing' turnaround in literacy at this primary school

children.content: An inspiring turnaround at an Aboriginal community school just outside Bunbury in south-west WA could provide a scalable solution for closing the gap on Indigenous literacy.
